data.title: Tandem gains as Truist becomes latest to upgrade on new pharmacy model
data.description:
Shares of Tandem Diabetes Care (TNDM) rose on Thursday as Truist upgraded the insulin pump maker to Buy from Hold, becoming the latest brokerage to raise its rating based on the company’s new pay-as-you-go pharmacy channel strategy.

On Tuesday, Piper Sandler upgraded the San Diego, California-based MedTech to Overweight from Neutral, arguing that the economic gains of the new strategy will improve Tandem’s (TNDM) revenue figures over the next three years.

In a research note on Thursday, Truist analyst Richard Newitter argued that Wall Street has yet to fully appreciate the growth trajectory of the company’s higher-margin recurring revenue as it transitions to the new model.

While analysts expect TNDM to face headwinds in 2026/27 as it pivots to the new model, which, unlike sales made through the DME channel, carries no upfront payments, he added that “we far prefer the economics of the Pay/GO model in the mid-term.”

Trading at ~1.3x in terms of 2027 revenue, the stock represents a compelling entry point as the Street underestimates the company’s growth and profitability prospects associated with converting patients to the new business model in 2027 and beyond, Newitter added.

He raised his price target on Tandem (TNDM) to $35 from $27 per share.

data.title: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac hit 52-week low as investors reportedly doubt IPO plans
data.description:
Investors are doubtful about the Trump administration's efforts to sell more stock in Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) to the public, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

The U.S. president was looking to offload about 5% of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac in a public offering in September last year.

But few concrete details have emerged since, and attention has seemingly shifted to other priorities, Bloomberg noted.

Both stocks have lost ~70% in the past six months, hitting their 52-week low today.

FNMA was down 7.99% to $4.26 during Thursday late morning, while FMCC was -8.37% to $3.72.

data.title: Mortgage rates rise for third straight week
data.description:
Mortgage rates increased for the third consecutive week, the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.22% as of March 19, up from 6.11% last week and down from 6.67% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.54%, up from 5.50% last week and below the year-ago 5.83%.

"The 30-year fixed-rate mortgage edged up this week to 6.22% but remains nearly half a percentage point lower than the same time last year," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist. 

"Potential homebuyers are poised for a more affordable spring homebuying season than last with the market experiencing improvements in purchase applications and pending home sales," added Khater.

data.title: BNP Paribas sees a Fed pivot to two-sided bias as yield risks build
data.description:
BNP Paribas is signaling a notable shift in the Federal Reserve’s policy trajectory, suggesting the central bank is moving toward a more balanced, two-sided stance following its March meeting.

According to a recent investor note by the bank, policymakers appear increasingly open to adjusting rates in either direction, depending on how key economic variables evolve in the coming months. 

The firm expects the Federal Open Market Committee to formally adopt a symmetric bias as early as its April meeting. Such a stance would indicate that both rate hikes and cuts are plausible outcomes, particularly if energy prices remain elevated while labor market conditions hold steady. 

Despite this shift in tone, BNP Paribas maintains its baseline view that monetary policy will remain unchanged through 2026. The bank notes that Fed Chair Jerome Powell has tied future rate cuts closely to clear evidence of disinflation—conditions it believes are unlikely to materialize this year. At the same time, the threshold for resuming rate hikes is also seen as high. 

In fixed income markets, BNP Paribas warns that U.S. Treasury yields are likely to face continued upward pressure. A combination of evolving Fed guidance, persistent fiscal concerns, and weakening swap spreads could amplify risks, potentially creating a feedback loop that drives yields higher. 

Fixed Income ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: Campbell's adds its iconic name to sauces
data.description:
The Campbell's Company (CPB) plans to continue to use its brand name for products apart from soup as its evolution continues. This June, the food company will launch a line of packaged sauces (garlic, smothered chicken, creamy lemon, butter chicken, and Thai curry) packaged in cans that have the iconic Campbell’s red and white lettering. 

The strategy shift started in 2024 when the company dropped “Soup” from its corporate name to rebrand as The Campbell’s Company (CPB). The goal was to reflect growth in sauces, snacks, and beverages, including brands like Rao’s and Prego. Executives have framed the branding change as retaining the iconic Campbell’s equity while signaling that sauces are now central to its identity, with soup needing only to stay flat as other categories drive incremental growth.

Looking ahead, the company has positioned Rao's as a key premium growth engine while leveraging the Campbell’s name so that consumers and retailers increasingly associate “Campbell's” not only with soup but with a portfolio of sauces across price tiers and occasions.

Shares of Campbell's (CPB) are down 45% on a year-to-date basis and trade just pennies off the 52-week low.

data.title: Honeywell debuts AI tool aimed at predicting industrial disruptions
data.description:
Honeywell (HON) on Thursday introduced an artificial intelligence system designed to help industrial operators anticipate equipment issues and respond to potential disruptions before they escalate.

The product, called Experion Operations Assistant, is built on the company’s Experion PKS control system and combines historical plant data with real-time inputs. The goal is to give operators earlier visibility into emerging risks tied to safety incidents or production losses.

 

 

The launch follows a pilot program involving companies including Chevron (CVX) and TotalEnergies (TTE) (TTEF). During testing, the system identified potential alarm events several minutes in advance, allowing operators to take corrective steps and reduce unplanned downtime.

Honeywell (HON) executives said the tool is intended to address operational challenges such as maintaining output from existing assets and managing the loss of experienced workers. The system uses plant-specific data and AI models to generate real-time guidance for control room staff.

Designed to integrate with existing infrastructure, the assistant analyzes site-level information and provides alerts intended to help operators respond more quickly to developing issues.

data.title: BP to sell German refinery, boosts cost savings target
data.description:
BP (BP) said Thursday it agreed to sell its Gelsenkirchen refinery in Germany to Klesch Group for an undisclosed sum and raised its cost reduction target, as the company continues to simplify its portfolio and shore up its balance sheet.

BP (BP) did not provide a value of the ​deal or the liabilities the sale would remove from its books, but Barclays analyst Lydia Rainforth estimated the latter in the $1.3B-$1.7B range.

The company said the sale of the refinery, ​which processes ~12M metric tons/year of crude oil, allowed it to increase its structural cost reduction target by ~$1B to $6.5B-$7.5B by 2027, which equates to ~30% of its 2023 cost baseline.

The deal will add to BP's (BP) free ​cash flow based ​on historical performance ⁠and will help lower the cash breakeven for its remaining refining portfolio, the company said.

The move follows an agreement in December to sell a 65% stake in its Castrol lubricants business for $8B; BP (BP) said last month that it had announced or completed more than $11B of its $20B divestment target for 2027.

data.title: Lucid lands just its second Wall Street bull rating as Citi points to upside
data.description:
Lucid Group (LCID) tracked higher in Thursday morning trading after Citi initiated coverage on the electric vehicle maker with a Buy rating. The firm sees momentum building for Lucid (LCID) as it pointed to Gravity model acceleration, Cosmos ramp, the partnership with PIF, and the ability to leverage the Uber (UBER) partnership. Those factors are seen as strong enough to place Lucid (LCID) on a path toward breakeven.

"We believe the company is at a positive inflection point, given the Gravity launch was completed in 4Q 25, the launch of the Cosmos is on track to begin production in 4Q 26, Uber robotaxi is expected to begin commercial operations by year-end, and the agreement will be expanded with an upsized volume commitment to include the Cosmos," highlighted analyst Michael Ward. He noted that Lucid (LICD) will begin offering monthly autonomous subscriptions in 2027, and European expansion will accelerate in 2026. 

In terms of financials, Citi believes Lucid (LCID) has liquidity through the second half of 2027, and additional capital market proceeds could clear an overhang and put the focus of investors squarely on product and technology momentum. After Lucid generated $1.4B in revenue in 2025, Citi expects 2026 revenue of $2.4B, revenue of $5.9B in 2027, and $9.2B in 2028 following the ramp-up and production of the lower-priced, midsized vehicles. 

Citi assigned a price target of $17 to Lucid (LCID). 

Notably, Citi's bull rating on Lucid (LCID) is only the second on Wall Street.

Shares of Lucid (LCID) moved 1.4% higher in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Fed chair staying on as governor would defy norms, Bessent sasy
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Thursday that Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell staying on the central bank's board as a governor after his chair term ends would be "outside of historic norms."

"Only one former chair has stayed on as a governor, and the president asked him to stay," Bessent said during an interview on Fox Business's "Mornings with Maria." "I can't imagine that President Trump would ask Chair Powell to stay on as a governor."

On Wednesday, Powell said he would stay on the Fed's Board of Governors as long as the Department of Justice's investigation into his handling of the Fed's building renovation project continues. He has not decided on whether he'll stay on after the legal issue is resolved.

Powell's term as chair ends in May, but his term as a Fed Governor doesn't end until January 2028.

When asked if President Donald Trump would drop the DOJ's investigation into Powell to advance the process to confirm Kevin Warsh as the next Fed chair, Bessent said that's U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro's decision.

"I'm not going to comment on an ongoing investigation," he said.  "We'll see how it goes."

He noted that the confirmation hearings could still proceed, but the vote would be delayed. Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) has vowed to block a vote on any Fed chair nominee until the DOJ investigation into Powell is resolved.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: MoonLake upgraded at Rothschild & Co Redburn citing potential FDA nod for lead drug
data.description:
Rothschild & Co Redburn upgraded MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (MLTX) to Buy from Neutral, citing a “clearer path to approval” for the Swiss biotech’s lead candidate, sonelokimab, targeted at a skin disorder known as hidradenitis suppurativa in the U.S.

Analyst Qize Ding argued that while regulatory filings for sonelokimab are not yet complete, its “biggest regulatory hurdle is out of the way" after, according to the company, the FDA said that no additional trials are required for the drug based on its existing Phase 2 and 3 trial data.

“Our base case is that SLK is unlikely to face major hurdles in the FDA review process and that its overall profile is likely to be similar to the best-in-class approved therapy in HS,” the analyst added, raising his price target on the stock to $40 from $12 per share.

Ding noted that MoonLake (MLTX) hasn’t changed its prior guidance on the timing for approval and launch of sonelokimab. He raised his peak sales estimates for the drug to $3.2B from $2.7B compared to $2.1B in the consensus, arguing that it “remains an attractive asset for large biopharma.”
